park_name: Discovery Parks - Fraser Street, Hervey Bay
rationale_top3: Exceptional family entertainment with two pools, jumping pillow, games room, tennis court, basketball, and go-karts, plus a standout natural lake with turtles, eels, ducks and fish that kids can feed. Staff-led activities including kids club, movie nights, breakfast gatherings, sausage sizzles, and live music ensure constant engagement. Outstanding cleanliness and presentation with spotless amenities, luxury-standard ensuites featuring waterfall showers and premium Ink & Water toiletries, and beautifully maintained grounds. Consistently described as immaculate across all facilities. Highly praised staff who go above and beyond with friendly, helpful service - from assisting with caravan positioning to providing first aid. Secure gated environment with key fob access provides peace of mind. Walking distance to Hervey Bay beach, esplanade, shops and restaurants.
rationale_honourable: Strong repeat visitor loyalty with many guests extending stays or immediately rebooking. The natural wildlife lake provides daily nature encounters that create memorable family experiences. Good value pricing with direct booking discounts and special deals available.
water_fun: Two pools (one heated), clean and inviting
kids_play: Jumping pillow, games room, tennis, basketball, go-karts
pet_detail: Dog friendly in caravans/campers/tents but not in cabins; pet-friendly ensuite sites available
best_for: This park is best suited for families with children under 10 who want wildlife interaction, water play, and active entertainment in a secure environment close to the beach.
executive_summary:
PARK: Discovery Parks - Fraser Street, Hervey Bay
SCORE: 82/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-24
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 175

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (17/20) — Exceptional family facilities with jumping pillow, games room, tennis, basketball, two pools, and go-karts creating constant engagement. The lake with turtles, ducks, eels and fish (free feed from reception) is a standout attraction. Staff-organized activities include kids club, movie nights, breakfast gatherings, sausage sizzles, live music, and even reptile shows. Minor points lost as games room described as "dated" and one pool occasionally closed.

Nature (16/20) — The natural lake with abundant wildlife (turtles, water birds, eels, fish) provides daily nature encounters that kids love. Beautiful landscaping with trees, green grass, and attractive gardens. Located 4-5 minute walk to Hervey Bay beach and esplanade with walking/cycling paths. Points lost for urban location (not bushland setting) and pigeon noise complaints from some guests.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Consistently praised as "spotless," "immaculate," and "meticulously clean" across amenities, ensuites, pools, and cabins. Ensuites described as luxury standard with waterfall showers and premium Ink & Water toiletries. Staff actively maintain grounds daily. One point lost for isolated incidents of BBQ area being "super filthy," mould in one cabin bathroom, and linen hygiene issue.

Value (13/15) — Generally considered good value with competitive pricing ($38/night for powered sites mentioned). Direct booking through website offers best rates. Special deals available (two weeks for price of one). Some guests felt prices were high compared to other parks on their journey, particularly given tight site sizes and construction disruptions during their stays.

Site Size (7/10) — Sites adequate for most rigs but consistently described as "tight," "cramped," and "narrow" especially for larger vans. Staff assistance offered and appreciated for positioning. Ensuite sites well-sized with level concrete pads. Some guests struggled for 30+ minutes to park due to narrow access roads and scaffolding from construction work.

Sentiment (9/10) — Overwhelmingly positive with numerous repeat visitors ("6th visit," "coming for 3 years," "2nd time," "our go to park"). Many guests extended stays or booked return visits during checkout. Staff praised extensively by name (Min, Ana, Amanda, John, Craig, Dave, Rhi). Minor points lost for handful of negative reception experiences and service issues.

Location (6/10) — Walking distance to beach, esplanade, shops, cafes, restaurants, and mini golf (4-5 minute walk). Short drive to supermarkets and marina. One street back from main esplanade provides quieter setting. Points lost as not beachfront location and some felt "back from beach" was disadvantage despite being close.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Site sizes are tight and cramped for larger caravans with narrow access roads, requiring staff assistance and causing parking struggles that lasted 30+ minutes for some guests.
2. Construction work during multiple visits created extreme noise, closed showers, blocked access with scaffolding, and covered vehicles in wood chips without warning or apology from management.
3. Location is one street back from beach rather than beachfront, and some isolated service issues included rude reception staff, poor refund communication, gate timer issues trapping guests, and poor TV/wifi reception.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"kids club and ample space to ride bikes," "turtles, eels and water fowl in the lake," "jumping pillow, games room, the 2 pools," "spotless amenities and very well maintained," "staff incredibly friendly and welcoming," "the cleanest cabins I have ever stayed in," "ensuite spotless and well appointed," "very secure and safe"

rationale_top3: Entertainment facilities (17/20): Strong entertainment offerings with activities and amenities for guests Nature & surroundings (14/20): Good natural setting and pleasant environmental features Cleanliness standards (13/15): Well-maintained facilities with high cleanliness standards
rationale_honourable: Value for money (12/15): Competitive pricing with good amenities for the cost
water_fun: Located in Hervey Bay with likely access to water-based activities and beach proximity
kids_play: Strong entertainment score suggests good children's facilities and play areas available
pet_detail: No pet facilities or pet-friendly accommodations based on zero pet score

rationale_top3: Exceptional cleanliness standards with amenities consistently rated among the best in Australia - 'cleanest facilities and best kept site we've seen on the whole trip' and perfect 15/15 cleanliness score Spacious, well-maintained sites suitable for large rigs with excellent security features including locked gates at night, creating a safe family environment Strong nature and location amenities including proximity to Botanical Gardens, 16km esplanade bike paths, and walking distance to major supermarkets, making it an ideal touring base for Fraser Island
rationale_honourable: Hervey Bay Caravan Park earns its Silver listing through exceptional cleanliness standards and maintenance that reviewers consistently praise as among the best they've encountered. While it lacks the waterpark-style entertainment that Gold-tier family parks offer, its combination of spacious sites, excellent security, pet-friendly facilities including an off-leash area, and proximity to supermarkets makes it a reliable choice for families seeking a clean, well-maintained base for exploring Hervey Bay and Fraser Island.
kids_play: Limited - pool and BBQ area only
pet_detail: Dog friendly park with dedicated off-leash fenced area on site, beautiful off-leash dog beach a few minutes drive away.
best_for: Families with young children who prioritize cleanliness, security, and a peaceful environment over on-site entertainment, ideal as a touring base for Fraser Island and Hervey Bay attractions.
executive_summary:
PARK: Hervey Bay Caravan Park
SCORE: 78/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-15
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 269

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (13/20) — Reviews mention a pool and BBQ area but limited kids entertainment facilities; one review noted "pool arcade for the kids" but most describe it as a quiet park with basic amenities rather than dedicated play equipment or activities; "Would not take kids here necessarily as no facilities other than a pool for entertainment"

Nature (16/20) — Located near beaches with walking distance access (15-20 minutes), adjacent to Botanical Gardens, access to 16km esplanade bike paths, off-leash dog beach nearby, Mary to Bay Rail trail; reviewers note "relaxing sound of birds singing" and "millions of flying foxes at dusk" but not waterfront location

Cleanliness (15/15) — Outstanding performance with consistent praise: "cleanest facilities and best kept site we've seen on the whole trip", "amenities are spotless", "immaculately clean", "amenities block is spotless", "we have never seen cleaner amenities blocks", "best I've seen on my trip around Australia"

Value (12/15) — Mixed feedback on pricing; some note "rates won't break the bank balance", others mention "too expensive" for coastal parks, "reasonably priced park", one reviewer complained about $57/week storage fees and found policies restrictive; generally seen as fair but not exceptional value

Site Size (9/10) — Consistently praised: "spacious sites", "huge sites", "sites are a generous size", "large flat sites", "generous sized camping and caravan bays", "plenty of room for our big rig"; some noted tightness for dual axle caravans but managers help with parking

Sentiment (9/10) — Very positive with high repeat visitation: "been going for the last 7 years", "27th year in this park", "stayed here many times", "will definitely be back", "would happily stay again"; some negative reviews about previous management but recent management (Sheree, Dave, Georgia) praised extensively

Location (4/10) — Not beachfront (15-20 minute walk to beach), described as "a bit far from things, you will need to drive", "a little out of the way", but close to Aldi and Woolworths (5 minute walk), McDonald's, KFC; "tucked away off the main road" and "quiet location"; good for Fraser Island tour pickups

WHY NOT 100:
1. Limited on-site kids entertainment facilities beyond a pool - no playground, waterpark, or structured activities programs that families with young children expect from top-tier holiday parks
2. Location is not beachfront and requires 15-20 minute walk or drive to reach the beach, reducing convenience for families with young children wanting easy beach access
3. Some reviews mention restrictive policies, strict rules, and past management issues that created unwelcoming experiences, plus inconsistent WiFi performance noted by several guests

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"cleanest facilities and best kept site we've seen on the whole trip", "amenities are spotless", "immaculately clean, great facilities", "spacious sites, lovely pool and great amenities", "walking distance to Aldi, Woolworths", "very secure with gates locked at night", "dog friendly with off leash area", "Would not take kids here necessarily as no facilities other than a pool"

rationale_top3: Exceptional cleanliness with amenities consistently described as 'spotlessly clean,' 'immaculate,' and 'pristine,' maintained daily by attentive staff (15/15 cleanliness score) Perfect location score (10/10) with walking distance to Urangan Pier, beachfront esplanade, Saturday markets, cafes, and off-leash dog beach, praised as 'close to everything' Strong natural coastal setting (16/20) offering beautiful sunset views, fishing opportunities, and beach access, creating an appealing family-friendly seaside atmosphere
rationale_honourable: Generous site sizes described as 'large' and 'spacious,' particularly appreciated by pet owners, though some tight maneuvering for larger vans (21ft+) Overwhelmingly positive sentiment with many repeat visitors calling it a 'traditional' annual family destination and 'best park by far' Good basic entertainment facilities including pool, playground, and games room with ping pong and arcade games that children enjoy
water_fun: Pool available, clean and appreciated by guests
kids_play: Playground plus games room with ping pong and arcade games
pet_detail: Dog friendly with designated pet area at back of park; off-leash beach nearby
best_for: Families with toddlers and young children who value immaculate facilities, safe secure grounds, and a short walk to the pier, beach, cafes, and Saturday markets
executive_summary:
PARK: Pier Caravan Park Hervey Bay
SCORE: 78/100
DATE SCORED: 2025-06-09
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 146

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (14/20) — Reviews mention a games room with ping pong and three arcade games, plus a playground for kids. Families appreciated these facilities, though there's no waterpark or extensive organised activities. One reviewer noted "Our son loved the games room with ping pong, arcade games" and another praised it as "a very kid friendly park." Entertainment is modest but adequate for younger children.

Nature (16/20) — The park is a short walk to the beautiful Urangan Pier, beachfront walking paths, and an off-leash dog beach. Multiple reviews highlighted "fantastic sunset views and fishing," "close to beach, esplanade," and "walking distance to the SPAR, bottle shop, pier and the market." The natural setting is coastal with easy access to water views, though not bushland or creek-based nature.

Cleanliness (15/15) — Overwhelmingly, reviews praised the park's cleanliness with phrases like "spotlessly clean facilities," "amenities were immaculate," "super clean," and "cleanest and most organised I have ever visited." Staff maintain the park daily and facilities are described as "pristine" and "top class."

Value (11/15) — Value perceptions were mixed. Several reviews noted "getting a little bit expensive" and "$62 a night pretty exy," while others said "great value" and "very clean, safe, and great value." Families appreciated fifth-night-free deals, but recent pricing concerns emerged in 2025-2026 reviews.

Site Size (8/10) — Most reviews praised "large sites," "spacious sites," and "big sites which is great when you have a dog." However, some noted challenges: "a bit tight for average backers," "tight to get into with a larger van," and "trees planted right where a large van needs to reverse." Sites are generally good but not universally easy for all rig sizes.

Sentiment (9/10) — Sentiment is overwhelmingly positive with many repeat visitors and high recommendation rates. Phrases like "will definitely be back," "our family have agreed that it is traditional to go to the Pier once a year," and "best park by far" dominate. However, three recent 1-star reviews (April 2026) cited aggressive staff behaviour and safety concerns, slightly tempering the sentiment score.

Location (10/10) — Location scored perfectly with consistent praise: "great location," "close to everything," "5 minute walk to the pier," "walking distance to town and Esplanade," and "close to the water, pier, great shops and eateries." Families loved the proximity to Urangan Pier, Saturday markets, cafes, and beaches.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Entertainment facilities are basic (playground and small games room) with no waterpark, jumping pillow, or extensive organised kids' activities that families expect from top-tier parks.
2. Value perceptions have declined in recent reviews with multiple families noting increasing costs and describing pricing as "expensive" or "exy," impacting affordability for budget-conscious families.
3. Site access issues for larger caravans (21ft+) were mentioned repeatedly, with poorly placed trees and tight angles making reversing difficult, and one reviewer damaged their wheel on a culvert.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"spotlessly clean facilities," "walking distance to everything we needed," "games room with ping pong arcade games," "close to Urangan Pier," "best park by far," "immaculately kept," "dog friendly," "very kid friendly park," "great location friendly staff," "getting a little bit expensive but it is worth it"

park_name: Fraser Coast Beachfront Tourist Parks | Scarness
rationale_top3: Nature & Location (28 points): Strong beachfront position in Hervey Bay with excellent natural surroundings and direct beach access Cleanliness (14 points): Well-maintained facilities and grounds consistently meeting guest expectations Value for Money (13 points): Competitive pricing for a beachfront location offering good amenities and coastal access
rationale_honourable: Entertainment facilities (8 points) provide basic recreational options, while adequate site sizing (8 points) offers functional accommodation space for families
water_fun: Direct beachfront access to Hervey Bay beaches for swimming, beach activities, and water recreation in the calm waters of the bay
kids_play: Basic play facilities available with the main attraction being the beach and outdoor coastal environment for family activities
pet_detail: Pets are not permitted at this park based on the zero pet score
best_for: Beach lovers, families wanting coastal access, whale watching season visitors, and those seeking a relaxed beachfront base to explore Hervey Bay

park_name: Fraser Coast Beachfront Tourist Parks | Torquay
rationale_top3: Outstanding beachfront location with direct beach access less than 10 meters from calm swimming waters, regular dolphin sightings, Fraser Island views, and walking distance to shops and cafes Consistently spotless and well-maintained amenities with daily cleaning, creating a hygienic family-friendly environment despite some older facilities Excellent value for money with beachfront powered sites around $56 per night, making premium coastal camping accessible for budget-conscious families
rationale_honourable: Fraser Coast Beachfront Tourist Parks | Torquay delivers an exceptional beachfront location with direct beach access that families consistently praise. The park's cleanliness standards are high, with spotless amenities and well-maintained grounds, though facilities show age in some areas. While the park excels in location and natural environment (calm swimming beach, dolphin sightings, beautiful sunsets), it lacks dedicated children's entertainment facilities and has notably tight site spacing, which prevents it from achieving Gold status for family holiday experiences.
water_fun: Pool and spa mentioned, calm beach swimming
kids_play: No dedicated playground, zooper doopers occasionally provided
best_for: Families with young children who love beach activities, simple camping experiences, and prioritize waterfront location and value over extensive resort-style facilities.
executive_summary:
PARK: Fraser Coast Beachfront Tourist Parks | Torquay
SCORE: 72/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-19
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 106

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (10/20) — Very limited on-site kids entertainment facilities; one reviewer mentioned managers occasionally providing zooper doopers for kids, but no playground, jumping pillow, or organized activities mentioned. The beach itself provides natural entertainment with dolphin sightings and calm swimming.

Nature (18/20) — Outstanding natural environment with absolute beachfront positioning less than 10 meters from the beach, calm ocean swimming, regular dolphin sightings, beautiful sunsets, and Fraser Island views. Direct beach access via stairs, great walking tracks along the esplanade, and natural bird sounds create an excellent coastal experience.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Consistently praised as "spotless," "very clean," and "sparkling clean facilities" with daily cleaning of amenities. A few reviews mentioned older/dated facilities and occasional issues (urine smell in toilets, washing machines not as clean), preventing a perfect score.

Value (13/15) — Generally excellent value with "$56 a night right next to the amenities" for ocean front views and "excellent price for powered sites" mentioned repeatedly. Some felt sites were "not worth the small site for price," and tighter spaces reduced perceived value for some guests.

Site Size (6/10) — Significant mixed feedback: some praised "sites are quite large" post-rebuild with "enough room for a large van, awning and parking," but many complained about being "packed in like sardines," "sites are pretty close together and quite small," tight reversing, and minimal car parking space, especially in back rows.

Sentiment (9/10) — Very strong positive sentiment with many repeat visitors ("3rd revisit," "over 30 years," "twice in 4 years"), enthusiastic recommendations, and high praise for management. A few negative experiences with booking errors and poor customer service prevented a perfect score.

Location (10/10) — Perfect score for location with absolute beachfront positioning, shops/supermarket across the road, cafes and Torquay pub within walking distance, patrolled beach nearby, surflifesaving club, and convenient Fraser Island tour bus pickup. Walking distance to all amenities.

WHY NOT 100:
1. No dedicated children's entertainment facilities — no playground, no kids' club, no jumping pillow, or organized activities that families with young children typically expect at holiday parks.
2. Site spacing issues create significant challenges — multiple reviews cite being "packed like sardines," difficulty reversing vans, minimal parking space, lack of privacy, and tight sites especially in back rows near the road.
3. Road noise from the esplanade — consistent complaints about "hoons" racing cars with loud exhausts, particularly at night around 9pm, with traffic noise intrusive for those in sites near the road.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Kids loved having the beach straight outside their camper", "absolute beachfront", "spotless amenities", "packed in like sardines", "dolphins playing right in front of us", "excellent price for powered sites", "came around with zooper doopers for the kids", "needs bigger sites", "right on the beach clean and friendly", "tight sites but manager is happy to help"

park_name: Fraser Coast Beachfront Tourist Parks | Burrum Heads
rationale_top3: Exceptional cleanliness with reviewers consistently describing amenities as immaculate and among the best they've seen, including recently renovated facilities with family bathrooms and daily cleaning schedules Outstanding natural setting right on the Burrum River with beautiful sunsets, immediate water access for fishing and water activities, and walking distance to swimming beach Perfect location with foodworks, bakery, butcher, and takeaway shops literally next door, plus spacious sites and highly praised management team
rationale_honourable: Fraser Coast Beachfront Tourist Parks at Burrum Heads has undergone a remarkable renovation, earning consistent praise for its immaculate amenities that reviewers describe as some of the best they've encountered. The beachfront location on the Burrum River, combined with spacious sites and exceptionally friendly management teams (particularly Sean, Kym, Nigel, and Fiona), creates a well-maintained environment that families return to year after year. While the park excels in cleanliness and natural setting, it lacks a pool and extensive structured kids' entertainment facilities, and some families may find the pricing on the higher end, though most agree you get what you pay for with the recent upgrades.
water_fun: River access for fishing and water activities
kids_play: Great playground mentioned, limited structured entertainment
pet_detail: Pet-friendly sites available in Southern section of park; dogs receive daily treats from management staff in designated dog-friendly areas
best_for: Families with young children who appreciate pristine facilities, beautiful natural surroundings, and a relaxed riverside atmosphere, particularly those who don't require extensive on-site kids' entertainment or a pool
executive_summary:
PARK: Fraser Coast Beachfront Tourist Parks | Burrum Heads
SCORE: 72/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-21
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 84

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — Reviews mention a "great playground" but no pool or structured kids' activities; one family noted "Would be great if park had a pool"; the park relies on natural environment rather than organized entertainment with no waterpark, kids' club, or activity programs mentioned.

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional natural setting "right on the Burrum river" with "beautiful sunsets" and "amazing sunsets and right on the water"; families enjoy river walks with "walk along the Burrum River is very pleasant"; beachfront sites offer "amazing views" and easy water access for fishing and SUP's; walking distance to swimming beach.

Cleanliness (15/15) — Outstanding cleanliness scores with "Best and cleanest toilet and shower facilities I've seen," "immaculate amenities and one of the best designs," "amenities spotless," and "not even a cobweb on the ceiling"; daily cleaning schedules maintained; recently renovated facilities described as "very fresh and clean."

Value (11/15) — Mixed feedback on pricing with "one of the pricier caravan parks we have stayed in" and "price is somewhat challenging but you get what you pay for"; one review noted "prices have increased by 38% per day"; however, many felt "Happy to pay the extra for a great stay" given the quality; $70 per night mentioned.

Site Size (7/10) — "Spacious sites" frequently mentioned with "large sites" and "generous grassed sites"; however, "Sites are very tight to get into at river side" and "roads between the sites are very narrow so it makes it difficult to get your van in"; back sites described as "roomier" for larger rigs.

Sentiment (9/10) — Very high satisfaction with families returning year after year; "16th year at this park" and "we love coming up this way every year"; strong recommendation rate with "If I could have 10 stars I would"; isolated negative reviews about management conflicts but overwhelmingly positive overall; "We will definitely be back" is a common refrain.

Location (10/10) — Perfect location marks with "Perfect location" and "location is excellent"; "Foodworks, bakery, butcher, take away fish and chips, fishing gear beside the park" all within walking distance; "literally next door" to shops; close to Hervey Bay attractions; river and beach access immediate.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of pool facilities which multiple families specifically noted would enhance the park, particularly for summer stays with children who don't have air conditioning in their vans.
2. Limited structured kids' entertainment beyond the playground, with no kids' club, organized activities, jumping pillow, or waterpark features that young families typically seek.
3. Pricing sits at the higher end of the market with some families noting significant price increases and others finding it "challenging," which affects value perception despite quality facilities.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Best and cleanest toilet and shower facilities I've seen", "immaculate amenities and one of the best designs", "right on the Burrum river", "beautiful sunsets", "family/parents bathroom which was sooo handy for bathing my 4 month old", "one of the pricier caravan parks", "Would be great if park had a pool", "If I could have 10 stars I would"

rationale_top3: Exceptional beachfront location with direct beach access described as a '10-second walk to the water', offering beautiful ocean views and sounds that create an outstanding coastal camping experience Recent major renovations have transformed facilities from tired and outdated to 'ridiculously clean and tidy' with 'next level' camp kitchens and sparkling amenities that now meet modern standards Prime position adjacent to council waterpark and within walking distance to shops, restaurants, and Hervey Bay attractions, providing excellent convenience for families despite limited on-site entertainment
rationale_honourable: Pialba Beach Tourist Park earns Silver recognition primarily for its exceptional beachfront location and the recent major upgrades that have transformed amenities from tired to top-quality. Families repeatedly praise the direct beach access, proximity to the adjacent waterpark, and the genuinely helpful staff who go above and beyond. While the park has historically struggled with small site sizes, outdated facilities, and some security concerns, the ongoing renovations appear to be addressing many longstanding issues, making this a park worth watching as improvements continue.
water_fun: Adjacent council waterpark, slides require payment
kids_play: Nearby waterpark and playground, minimal on-site
pet_detail: Recently became dog-friendly in newly refurbished sections
best_for: This park best suits families with young children who want direct beach access and waterpark proximity without needing extensive on-site entertainment or large caravan sites.
executive_summary:
PARK: Pialba Beach Tourist Park
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2024-01-14
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 96

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (12/20) — Limited on-site entertainment with no resort-style facilities; main attraction is the adjacent council waterpark where slides cost $8 for 10 rides. Reviews mention pool table and safety supervision at waterpark, but note "site lacks any guest quality guest entertainment." Staff made Christmas special for kids in one instance, but no structured kids club or activities programs mentioned.

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional beachfront location with direct beach access described as "literally a 10-second walk to the water." Multiple reviews praise waking up to ocean sounds and beautiful beach views at low and high tide. Walking tracks along esplanade, mud flats exploration, and natural environment with lorikeets and brolgas. Minor deductions for fruit bat issues under certain trees and road noise.

Cleanliness (13/15) — Reviews show significant improvement post-renovation with recent guests praising "ridiculously clean and tidy" amenities and "sparkling" facilities. However, older reviews from 2023-2024 frequently mentioned "outdated," "tired," "broken toilets," "asbestos warnings," and inconsistent hot water. Ongoing renovations appear to be addressing historical cleanliness issues effectively.

Value (12/15) — Mixed feedback with some praising "very well priced cheaper than anyone else" at $60/night with services, while others felt sites were overpriced for their small size. Waterpark access requires additional payment contrary to some expectations. Strict cancellation policy taking 75% of deposit noted negatively. Good value for location but site size concerns affect perception.

Site Size (6/10) — Consistent criticism across reviews for "very small beach side spots," with multiple families noting difficulty fitting tent plus car, or needing "to book 2 sites" for adequate space. Newer upgraded section offers bigger sites for large caravans, but overall park described as "packed in" with sites "quite close together." Some dirt sites with poor positioning under trees.

Sentiment (9/10) — Strong repeat visitation with many "definitely coming back" and "our go to camping spot" comments. Staff consistently praised as "super friendly," "extremely helpful," and "accommodating." However, sentiment impacted by theft concerns, some unwelcoming experiences, discrimination mentioned by one immigrant family, and frustration with booking errors. Overall positive but not universally excellent.

Location (10/10) — Outstanding location marks with beachfront position, walking distance to shops (5 min drive to supermarkets, "short walk to Main Street"), restaurants, cafes, and direct waterpark access. "Perfect location," "couldn't get any better," "drop dead location" repeatedly mentioned. Easy access to Hervey Bay attractions, whale watching departure points, and esplanade bike paths.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Site sizes are consistently criticized as too small for families with caravans, tents, and vehicles, requiring some to book two sites or struggle with cramped positioning, particularly impacting the family camping experience.

2. Historical lack of on-site entertainment facilities and activities for children, with families dependent on the adjacent paid waterpark and external attractions rather than resort-style amenities within the park itself.

3. Security and theft concerns mentioned across multiple reviews including stolen bikes, food, and towels, with warnings to lock everything up, creating anxiety for families and single travelers despite staff being lovely.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"amenities ridiculously clean and tidy, everything was brand new," "camp kitchen is next level, easily one of the best," "right on the beachfront, literally a 10-second walk," "very small beach side spots," "kids had so much fun, perfect spot right on the beach," "theft issue at this park, way to feel safe," "currently undergoing renovations," "perfect for families"

rationale_top3: Inconsistent cleanliness standards with multiple recent reports of filthy cabins, mould, and poor maintenance despite many praising cleanliness. Limited on-site entertainment and activities for children beyond basic games room and pool—lacks holiday atmosphere and structured kids' programs. Tight, small sites causing difficulty for caravans, combined with distance from beach requiring car/significant walk, reducing convenience and space families expect.
rationale_honourable: Tasman Holiday Parks - Fraser Coast delivers a solid, well-maintained caravan park experience with consistently clean amenities and friendly staff that families appreciate. While it lacks standout entertainment facilities and has variable cabin quality, the park's quiet atmosphere, functional pool, games room, and excellent camp kitchen make it a reliable choice for families seeking affordable accommodation near Hervey Bay's attractions.
water_fun: Pool available, described as nice and refreshing
kids_play: Games room and common area for children
pet_detail: Pet friendly with small dogs allowed in some cabins; dogs receive welcome gift on arrival.
best_for: This park suits families with young children looking for affordable, clean accommodation with decent facilities who plan to spend days exploring Hervey Bay and Fraser Island rather than staying on-site.
executive_summary:
PARK: Tasman Holiday Parks - Fraser Coast
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-21
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 96

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (12/20) — Reviews mention games area, common room for kids, pool with pool cues available, and BBQ areas, but limited structured activities or entertainment programs. Families appreciated kids having space to play but noted lack of holiday atmosphere compared to resort-style parks.

Nature (12/20) — Park described as quiet and well-maintained with lovely gardens, but 15-20 minute walk to beach/esplanade. No creek, bushland or significant natural features on-site. Location serves as base for Fraser Island tours but nature experience requires leaving the park.

Cleanliness (11/15) — Strong mixed feedback. Many praised "exceptionally clean" amenities, "spotless" camp kitchen, and clean cabins. However, multiple recent reviews reported filthy cabins, mould, hair in showers, stained crockery, and ant infestations, indicating inconsistent cleaning standards.

Value (10/15) — Mixed reviews on value. Some noted "outstanding value," "good price," and "very good value for money." Others complained of overpriced cabins at $200-362/night that were "tiny," "basic," and "least value for money park during our 6 week trip."

Site Size (6/10) — Multiple complaints about "tiny" and "small sites," difficulty backing in vans, "tight" spaces with vans on either side. Several reviews mentioned sites had "a lot of room" but negative comments about cramped conditions were more common.

Sentiment (8/10) — Generally positive with many 5-star reviews and repeat visitors. Families praised friendly staff and quiet atmosphere. However, significant negative reviews about cleanliness, value, and misleading photos. Some stated they won't return.

Location (9/10) — Centrally located between shops and beach, 10-15 minute walk to esplanade, 10 minutes to shopping centre. Close to Fraser Island tour departure. Main road noise noted by some. Convenient but not beachfront.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Inconsistent cleanliness standards with multiple recent reports of filthy cabins, mould, and poor maintenance despite many praising cleanliness.
2. Limited on-site entertainment and activities for children beyond basic games room and pool—lacks holiday atmosphere and structured kids' programs.
3. Tight, small sites causing difficulty for caravans, combined with distance from beach requiring car/significant walk, reducing convenience and space families expect.

Minimal on-site entertainment and kid-friendly facilities - the games room was frequently closed, dirty, or non-functional, with nothing for the kids to do forcing families into constant day trips, contradicting the BIG4 family holiday park promise and leaving children disappointed. The magnesium pool is a highlight and the location offers beach access within walking distance. However, the park is undergoing significant renovations with dated amenities, small cramped sites, and a high number of permanent residents that detract from the holiday atmosphere families expect from a BIG4 property.
water_fun: Magnesium pool highlight, lacks shade coverage
kids_play: Basic playground, games room often closed/dirty
pet_detail: Dog friendly with off-leash beach nearby, but some reports of confrontational staff regarding pet policies and dogs must stay in cabins at all times in some cases.
best_for: Budget-conscious couples or retirees with self-contained rigs seeking a quiet location away from tourist crowds, who can overlook dated facilities during the park's transition period.
executive_summary:
PARK: BIG4 Hervey Bay Holiday Park
SCORE: 58/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-15
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 270

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (10/20) — Reviews consistently mention limited on-site entertainment with a basic games room that was frequently "dirty", "not functional", or closed and relocated to a "tin shed" during renovations. The pool table was often out of order, and families reported "nothing for the kids to do" on site, requiring day trips for activities. A few positive mentions of staff-organized sausage sizzles and Easter egg hunts, but insufficient for typical BIG4 family expectations.

Nature (12/20) — The park offers walking distance beach access ("2 minutes away", "5 minute walk") but reviewers note it's rocky with "not a lot of sand" and "not great for swimming". Located in a residential area away from main Hervey Bay attractions, with some tree coverage and bird life mentioned positively. The natural environment is pleasant but not outstanding, with the bay and esplanade walks being the primary nature drawcard.

Cleanliness (9/15) — This scored poorly with deeply divided opinions. Many recent reviews cite "dirty pool", "cabin floor was disgusting", "toilets and showers not very clean with grout and tiles looking very dirty and mouldy", "amenities old, inadequate and dirty", and "expired milk" in cabins. However, some guests found facilities "spotless", "always clean", and "very clean amenities". The inconsistency and high volume of negative cleanliness feedback, particularly regarding amenities and cabins, significantly impacts this score.

Value (8/15) — Families felt the park was overpriced for what's delivered, with reviews stating "bit pricey", "very expensive for what it is", "$80 for a tiny unpowered", and "price you pay is the same like you are staying at a normal big 4" despite being "not a modern Big 4". Dynamic pricing was criticized ("charged $85 despite website saying $63"). Some found it "really cheap rates" and "excellent value" but majority of family reviewers felt disappointed given BIG4 expectations versus delivery.

Site Size (6/10) — Significant criticism about site size with reviews stating "sites are small is an understatement", "too close to each other", "very small with very narrow roads", "tight parking", "site was a bit tight", and "extremely tight parking our ute". Multiple reviews mention needing staff assistance to reverse into sites. Some praised "spacious sites" and concrete slabs, but the overwhelming evidence points to cramped conditions for larger modern rigs.

Sentiment (6/10) — Mixed sentiment with passionate advocates (repeat visitors praising staff like Craig) versus families who "left after 1 night", "left after 2 and booked somewhere else", "left straight away", or "wouldn't book again". Many questioned "how they got the big 4 name" and called it "worst BIG4 we've stayed at". The high number of permanent residents created a "trailer park vibe" that detracted from the holiday feel families seek. Staff received consistent praise but facilities disappointed.

Location (7/10) — Located "a little bit out of town" in Point Vernon, "at the wrong end of town", "12 minutes from centre", making it "too far to walk to main shops" but offering a "quiet location" away from hustle. Beach access within walking distance is positive, but families noted it's "far away from beach" (rocky beach, not main swimming beach) and "not close to shops or cafes". The quieter residential setting suited some but disappointed families expecting proximity to Hervey Bay's main attractions.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Severely dated and inadequately maintained amenities with persistent cleanliness issues - multiple reviews cite dirty showers, mouldy tiles, scummy toilets, broken washers/dryers, and cabins requiring "desperate scrub or new flooring", failing to meet basic BIG4 standards that families expect and pay premium prices for.

2. Minimal on-site entertainment and kid-friendly facilities - the games room was frequently closed, dirty, or non-functional, with "nothing for the kids to do" forcing families into constant day trips, contradicting the BIG4 family holiday park promise and leaving children disappointed.

3. Very tight, cramped sites unsuitable for modern family rigs with high permanent resident population creating a "trailer park vibe" rather than a holiday atmosphere - sites described as "too close", "small is an understatement", with difficult access requiring staff parking assistance, and the permanent resident presence (some described as "rude" and "grumpy") detracted significantly from the family holiday experience.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"not a modern Big 4", "amenities are older style BUT are being upgraded", "nothing for the kids to do", "sites are small is an understatement", "Craig is a true legend", "dirty pool, terrible old permanent vans", "worst BIG4 we've stayed at", "left after 1 night", "magnesium pool was a highlight", "staff are amazing, friendly and helpful", "construction site towards the front", "do NOT expect Big4 quality"

rationale_top3: No dedicated playground or structured entertainment for young children - families with younger kids consistently disappointed by lack of play facilities, with games room typically occupied by older children Inconsistent cleanliness standards particularly in cabins - while amenities praised as spotless, multiple reports of filthy deluxe cabins with mould, hair, food scraps, ant infestations and poor maintenance Very tight caravan sites unsuitable for larger rigs - sites described as tiny with only 1-1.5 metres clearance, making reversing nearly impossible and creating cramped conditions for families
rationale_honourable: Despite limitations, the park delivers solid fundamentals: exceptional camp kitchen facilities described as the best guests have seen, genuinely clean amenities blocks, prime central location within 10-15 minute walk to Esplanade and shops, spacious cabins (when clean), well-maintained pool, and friendly welcoming staff who create a comfortable base for exploring Hervey Bay attractions at reasonable prices
water_fun: Pool well-maintained, clean and popular with guests
kids_play: Games room only, no playground for young kids
pet_detail: Pet-friendly cabins and sites available with conditions; small dogs welcome with goodie bag on arrival
best_for: Families with older children who prioritize cleanliness, location near the beach, and a well-equipped camp kitchen over on-site kids entertainment
executive_summary:
PARK: Tasman Holiday Parks - Hervey Bay
SCORE: 58/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-22
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 103

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — Reviews consistently note there is NO playground for young children, which was "a let down for us with younger kids." A games room and BBQ area exist, with mentions of "games area and common room" and "Pool, kid's area, BBQ" but the games area was "usually occupied by older kids with attitudes." No waterpark, jumping pillow, or organized kids activities mentioned.

Nature (10/20) — The park is centrally located "between shops and beach" with the "beautiful esplanade a short 5 minutes walk away" and "1km walk to beach." However, the park itself offers limited natural environment, with no mention of bushland, creeks, or significant green space. The location is "20 minutes from the beach" by some accounts, suggesting distance varies by cabin/site placement.

Cleanliness (10/15) — Mixed evidence with strong recent positives but concerning negatives. Many reviews praise "exceptionally clean," "spotless," "one of the cleanest campsite bathrooms I've ever seen," and "clean amenities" maintained throughout stays. However, multiple family reviews report serious issues: "Deluxe Cabin was Filthy," "hair all over the shower walls, mould in the shower, food scraps between cushions," "dirty, tiles chipped cracked, stains on floor," "ant infestation," and "wasn't cleaned very well as there were bits of dirt on the floor."

Value (9/15) — Generally positive for budget accommodation with "outstanding value for money," "good price," "reasonable price," and "very good value for money" mentioned frequently. However, families paid premium prices during peak with poor experiences: "$362 per night" described as "overpriced tiny cabin," "almost $200 a night" was "an absolute disappointment," and one reviewer felt it was "the least value for money park during our 6 week trip. Expensive, small site."

Site Size (5/10) — Significant concerns for caravans and families. Multiple reviews report "sites are tiny," "small site," "very tight row," "sites are small and access is pretty tight," "about 1.5 metres to a tent next door and about 1 metre to a camper trailer on the other side," and "just a little tight trying to park van." Cabins described as "spacious" and "very spacious" but caravan sites clearly cramped.

Sentiment (7/10) — Polarized between loyal repeaters and disappointed families. Strong positive: "home away from home," "we will be back," "would gladly stay here again," "already booked again for next Christmas." However, recent family reviews show frustration: "staff couldn't care less," "we won't be back," "would not recommend this park," "I will probably look for somewhere else to stay," and serious billing disputes with "still scammers!!!"

Location (9/10) — Consistently praised as "central location," "close to everything," "centrally located between shops and beach," "great location for the esplanade - only 15 minute walk away," "walking distance to the beachfront and shops," and "10 minutes walk to Stocklands shopping centre." Main negative is road noise: "backed onto a main road which was very noisy," "road noise is terrible," and "road noise is pretty full on too and runs through the night."

WHY NOT 100:
1. **No playground or structured kids entertainment** — Multiple family reviews explicitly state "no playground for kids which was a let down for us with younger kids" and the games room was "usually occupied by older kids," leaving young families with limited on-site activities beyond the pool.

2. **Inconsistent cleanliness with serious cabin issues** — While amenities are praised, multiple families reported filthy cabins with "black mould in bathroom," "hair all over the shower walls," "food scraps between cushions," "ant infestation," and stains, suggesting cleaning standards vary significantly between accommodation types.

3. **Very tight sites unsuitable for larger rigs** — Caravan sites are repeatedly described as "tiny," with "about 1.5 metres" clearance, making it "nearly impossible" to reverse without hitting something, and "lots of permanents" occupying the better spaces, making this challenging for family travellers with caravans.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"no playground for kids which was a let down", "games area was usually occupied by older kids", "best camp kitchen I've ever come across", "one of the cleanest campsite bathrooms I've ever seen", "sites are tiny", "Deluxe Cabin was Filthy", "15 minute walk away to esplanade", "staff couldn't care less", "road noise is pretty full on", "outstanding value for money", "we will be back", "would not recommend this park"

rationale_top3: Nature & surroundings score of 14/20 indicates a pleasant natural setting typical of the Hervey Bay area Value for money score of 10/15 suggests reasonable pricing for the facilities offered Cleanliness score of 10/15 reflects acceptable maintenance standards across the park
rationale_honourable: Location score of 9/15 shows good proximity to Hervey Bay attractions Entertainment score of 8/15 provides basic recreational facilities for families Pet-friendly score of 7/10 indicates accommodating policies for guests with animals

rationale_top3: Outstanding beachfront location just 50m from the beach with walking distance to Hervey Bay shops, cafes, restaurants and esplanade - consistently praised as the park's strongest feature Friendly, accommodating management team (especially Natalie) who receive regular praise from repeat visitors for their helpful and welcoming service Well-maintained heated pool and spa facilities providing good water-based recreation options for families
rationale_honourable: Fraser Coast Holiday Park offers an excellent beachfront location in Hervey Bay, just 50m from the beach and walking distance to shops and cafes. The managers, particularly Natalie and her team, receive consistent praise for their friendly, accommodating service. However, significant concerns about cleanliness, maintenance issues, congested sites, and inconsistent cabin quality prevent this park from achieving a higher rating for families with young children.
kids_play: Rec room with pool table, arcade
pet_detail: Pet friendly with dogs allowed in designated cabins and secured in park at night with gates

executive_summary:
PARK: Fraser Coast Holiday Park
SCORE: 52/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-15
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 60

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (9/20) — Limited on-site kids entertainment with reviews mentioning only a recreation room containing pool table, arcade machine, television, board games and books; no dedicated playground or structured kids activities noted.
Nature (14/20) — Excellent beach proximity at just 50m from the beach with walking distance to esplanade; location praised consistently but limited natural environment within the park itself with grassy sites and some trees mentioned.
Cleanliness (7/15) — Highly polarized reviews with recent feedback citing "old, dirty, rundown" facilities, "FILTHY couch", "bed bugs", "dog hair all over blankets", while some positive reviews mention clean amenities; significant maintenance issues including broken power points, leaky roofs, and worn flooring.
Value (7/15) — Mixed feedback with some praising "great value", but concerns about "huge variations in prices" with different guests charged vastly different rates ($25 vs $59 for same sites); pricing inconsistencies and unexpected charges mentioned.
Site Size (4/10) — Consistent complaints about "extremely small and tight" parking, "little tight for big rigs", "small sites" with caravans/campervans "quite close to each other"; congested layout particularly problematic for families with larger vans.
Sentiment (5/10) — Divided sentiment with loyal repeat visitors praising management (especially Natalie), but numerous 1-star reviews from families citing noise issues, poor soundproofing, management disputes, and disappointment versus website photos; limited family-specific positive feedback.
Location (9/10) — Outstanding location just 50m to beach, walking distance to shops, cafes, restaurants, pubs and esplanade; backs onto bowls club; consistently praised as "perfect location" and "centrally located".

WHY NOT 100:
1. Severe cleanliness and maintenance issues reported in multiple recent reviews including bed bugs, filthy cabins, broken fixtures, worn flooring, and facilities described as "run down" and "disgusting" - critical concerns for families with young children.
2. Extremely limited on-site entertainment for children with only a basic rec room mentioned; no playground, waterpark, kids club, or structured activities that families with young children would expect from a quality holiday park.
3. Very tight, congested sites causing parking difficulties and lack of space, with poor soundproofing in accommodations leading to noise complaints - particularly problematic for families needing space and quiet for young children.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Only 50m to the beach", "The managers are absolute legends", "pool and spa awesome", "recreational room is very family friendly", "Old, dirty, rundown", "bed bugs. This place is disgusting", "parking onsite is extremely small and tight", "cabins need maintenance and cleaning", "Facilities need repairs", "walking distance to beach and nice pubs and cafes"

executive_summary:
PARK: Harbour View Caravan Park
SCORE: 42/100
DATE SCORED: 2024-03-15
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 26

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (2/20) — Multiple recent reviews indicate the pool has safety tape around it and is falling apart, with one visitor stating "pool lets not go there." No evidence of kids' club, playground, waterpark, or organized activities for children.

Nature (12/20) — Located in Hervey Bay with harbour views and proximity to Fraser Island (K'gari) boat access. Several reviews mention the location is ideal for Fraser Island tours and whale watching excursions, with marina adjacent. Not a natural bushland or beach-front setting.

Cleanliness (6/15) — Mixed feedback with recent reviews noting "toilets are cleaned every day" but also "Everything is falling apart," "Old and tired facilities," and one detailed 2023 review describing "camp kitchen does NOT get attended to and is broken, filthy." Grounds described as "not well looked after."

Value (10/15) — Consistently noted as "the cheapest park in town" with rates around $45-60 per night. Reviews acknowledge "great rates" but question value with comments like "Price $60 for what!!" Several note it's acceptable for the low price but facilities don't match expectations.

Site Size (8/10) — Positive mentions of "Huge camp-sites" and "plenty of room," with sites described as "easy to get in to." One review noted difficulty parking big rigs, but overall space receives favorable comments.

Sentiment (4/10) — Recent 2025-2026 reviews heavily weighted show declining satisfaction with multiple 1-star reviews citing rude staff, management issues, and one family cancelling due to feeling unwelcome. Earlier reviews more positive but trend is concerning. Several "would not return" statements.

Location (8/10) — Excellent positioning next to boat harbour and marina with "1 minute walk" to boat club and easy Fraser Island tour access. However, noted as "a bit far from the actual town" and noise complaints about adjacent fish co-op affecting amenity.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of family entertainment facilities with pool in disrepair and safety tape visible, providing no recreational options for children staying at the park.
2. Significant cleanliness and maintenance issues with facilities described as "very run down," "old and tired," and "falling apart" across multiple recent reviews, indicating poor upkeep.
3. Concerning customer service problems with multiple recent reviews citing rude staff who hang up on customers, unwelcoming management, and a family cancelling because they "didn't feel comfortable bringing our family" due to being made to feel like an inconvenience.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"amenities dated, pool lets not go there", "Everything is falling apart. Safety tape around the pool", "Old and tired facilities", "didn't feel comfortable bringing our family", "toilets are cleaned every day", "Huge camp-sites", "cheapest park in town", "very run down", "noise from the adjacent fish co-op", "a lot of permanent residents"
